| was born in summer, in a town called
Matatiele in the Eastern Cape. Matatiele
is near Lesotho where there are many
mountains. In the winter they are covered
with snow.

My father was a builder and he moved
from place to place to find work. We
moved to Lesotho and that's where |
started school. | was very happy. But
when I was eleven years old, | grew tired
and weak, and then | couldn't walk. My
parents took me to the hospital.
What is wrong,
doctor?
| don't know. We
must do some special
tests to find out.

J
| had to stay in hospital for three years.
| was very tired and I slept many hours
every day. After three years the doctors
told me | would not be able to walk again.
| was very sad because | love sports.
We think Zanele has
got TB in her spine. It has
attacked her nerves. | am
very sorry, but she will be in
a wheelchair for the rest of
her life. yy

| went to a special school in Umtata, and
my teachers encouraged me. It was a
very busy school, with lots of activities
like sport and sewing. On weekends we \\ we 71
had tasks to do. We had to clean our /

| decided to keep trying, and never give
up. If you have a disability, you can still
do something you love. | love to see how
far | can go, so | entered a competition. |
won a bronze medal for shot putt, a silver
medal for discus and a gold medal for
javelin.

Well done, Zanele - you
are really improving!

| entered more competitions and | got
many medals and prizes. | started to
dream about maybe competing in the
Paralympics one day.

The Paralympics are just like the But in the Paralympics, the athletes are

Then | heard that | was going to Sydney ee
in Australia, to take part in the 2000 Yo boo
Paralympics. At last we were in the plane, | «alt SO
. . \ excited!
flying over the Indian Ocean!

But when | got there, | suddenly felt very
scared of all the people watching me. |
just wanted to go home again.
Feo Ae ear

Then, when I threw my javelin it landed
far in front of everyone's. | broke the
world record and | got a gold medal! |
was the first African woman to win a gold
medal in the Paralympics. | was so happy
and very proud.

My motto is: Keep going, follow your dreams
and never give up. Even if something —_
happens to make you sad or scared.
That is what I teach my little girl, AZamazi.
———_ She is eight years old and she also loves to
do athletics and gymnastics.
lam still training and going to competitions,
and in 2016 | hope to go to the Paralympics
in Rio de Janeiro.
